attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure displays two side-by-side 3D coordinate frames (a and b) where the vertical axis (Imaginary) points straight up, the horizontal-right axis (Real) points up and right, and the third axis (n) points down and right. The curves (helices) spiral along the n axis, seen from a front-left, slightly elevated perspective.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot shows two 3D systems side-by-side, but the camera is positioned and zoomed such that Panel (a) is heavily clipped off-screen to the left, and Panel (b) is positioned towards the right side. The viewing angle has the n axis pointing almost directly away and right, creating a different foreshortening effect.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The rendered camera framing is highly incorrect, causing Panel (a) to be cut off at the left boundary of the image. The elevation and azimuth angles are slightly off, making the curves look squashed compared to the open, elegant spirals in the source figure.
discrepancies: Panel (a) is almost entirely cut off/clipped on the left edge of the rendered viewport.
The 3D cones on the axes are too large and bulky compared to the simple, sharp 2D arrowheads in the source.
The tube geometry representing the helix is too thick and has visible segment faceting rather than being a smooth, solid line.
The labels use 'ℑ' and 'ℜ' gothic symbols instead of the double-struck '𝕀' and 'ℝ' symbols shown in the source.
The overall composition is extremely zoomed out and poorly framed, leaving massive white space in the center-left and clipping the actual graphics.
evaluation.notes: The mathematical plotting of the complex exponential is correct, but the rendering is severely compromised by poor camera framing, clipping of Panel (a), and lack of UI interactions.
action_items: Adjust the camera position, target, and zoom in `setCameraView` so that both Panel (a) and Panel (b) are perfectly centered, balanced, and fully visible without clipping.
Smooth out the helix curves by increasing the segment radial/tubular segments in TubeGeometry.
Add a simple UI slider or step control to let the user change the frequency parameter 'k' or animate the sample index 'n'.
Change the 'ℑ' and 'ℜ' labels to double-struck letters '𝕀' and 'ℝ' (or simple bold serif 'I' and 'R') to match the textbook's style.
Scale down the 3D axis cones to make them look more like sleek mathematical arrows.

feedback.rationale: The rendering has severe camera framing issues with Panel (a) clipped, scale discrepancies on the axes/helix, and lacks required interactivity, all of which are addressable by the generator.
action_items: Adjust the camera position, target, and zoom in `setCameraView` so that both Panel (a) and Panel (b) are perfectly centered, balanced, and fully visible without clipping.
Smooth out the helix curves by increasing the segment radial/tubular segments in TubeGeometry.
Add a simple UI slider or step control to let the user change the frequency parameter 'k' or animate the sample index 'n'.
Change the 'ℑ' and 'ℜ' labels to double-struck letters '𝕀' and 'ℝ' (or simple bold serif 'I' and 'R') to match the textbook's style.
Scale down the 3D axis cones to make them look more like sleek mathematical arrows.

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure shows two panels, (a) and (b), each representing a 3D plot with coordinate axes: vertical imaginary axis, horizontal-right real axis, and a depth-pointing sample index axis 'n'. The viewpoint is slightly elevated and from the right, looking down at the helices.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot matches this perspective very well, displaying both panels with identical orthographic camera settings, viewing the helices from an elevated, right-of-center perspective.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: The viewing angles are highly consistent, capturing the same elevation and azimuth to correctly replicate the 3D helical structure relative to the axes.

shape_comparison.rendered_shape: black spheres with brown line crosses

discrepancies: The real and imaginary axis labels use gothic script symbols (ℜ, ℑ) instead of the blackboard bold symbols (ℝ, 𝕿/𝕀) shown in the original figure.
The crosses on the sample dots are colored brown/orange instead of black.
On panel (a), the label '1' near the Real axis slightly overlaps with the helix curve.
The font style for '1' and '-1' ticks is standard serif instead of matching the textbook math font exactly.
evaluation.notes: Excellent mathematical accuracy and geometry construction, but lacks interactive UI controls (sliders/buttons) and uses gothic instead of blackboard bold letters.
action_items: Change the axis labels 'ℜ' and 'ℑ' to blackboard bold 'ℝ' and '𝕀' to match the source figure.
Change the color of the sample cross lines from brown (0xb46a00) to black (0x000000) to match the source dots.
Adjust label offsets to prevent overlaps, particularly the '1' label near the Real axis on panel (a).
Add interactive parameter controls (such as a slider to vary 'k' or 'N' in real-time) to improve the interactivity score.

feedback.rationale: While geometry is excellent, the figure lacks standard interactive controls (sliders/buttons) and has fixable cosmetic issues with axis labels, colors, and overlapping text.

Add interactive parameter controls (such as a slider to vary 'k' or 'N' in real-time) to improve the interactivity score.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

evaluation.viewing_angle_source: The source figure depicts two 3D coordinate systems with the imaginary axis pointing vertically, the real axis pointing up-right, and the sequence index axis pointing down-right. The viewpoint is elevated and looking from the left-front, rendering the helical structures in clear isometric-like perspective.
evaluation.viewing_angle_rendered: The rendered screenshot matches the elevated orthographic perspective well. The vertical imaginary axis, diagonal real axis, and diagonal sequence index axis align closely with the source figure's layout.
evaluation.viewing_angle_comparison: Both viewing angles are highly consistent, utilizing a matching orthographic elevation and azimuth to render the twin panels side-by-side.
discrepancies: The label 'n' in panel (a) appears corrupted or overlapped with other text.
The tick marks/crosses on the black sample spheres are brown instead of black.
The label '1' overlaps slightly with the 'R' label in panel (a).
evaluation.notes: Excellent side-by-side reconstruction with functional wave parameters, though text overlaps slightly clutter the visual presentation.
action_items: Increase the visual offset of labels to prevent overlapping of '1' and 'R'.
Resolve the text rendering glitch of the 'n' axis label in panel (a).
Change the color of the sample crosses from brown to black to align with the original aesthetic.

feedback.rationale: The figure has high scores across geometry, interactivity, and faithfulness, and the remaining issues are minor label overlaps and color details that do not compromise the overall quality or conceptual clarity.
action_items: Increase the visual offset of labels to prevent overlapping of '1' and 'R'.
Resolve the text rendering glitch of the 'n' axis label in panel (a).
Change the color of the sample crosses from brown to black to align with the original aesthetic.
